task: Write a Python function kth_ordered(words, k) returning the k-th word, counting from 1, when the words are ordered by increasing length, and words of equal length are ordered reverse-alphabetically among themselves. Return None when k is out of range. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The solution is correct, meets the conciseness requirement, and mentions an edge case.

trace: To solve this task, I used the built-in `sorted()` function in Python with a custom key function that considers both word length and reverse alphabetical order. By using enumerate(), I was able to count the position of each word as it is sorted. This way, the k-th word can be easily retrieved from the sorted list. To handle the edge case when k is out of range, I added a condition to check if k is within the valid range (1 <= k <= len(sorted_words)). If not, the function returns None.
